summary:
Metatarsus adductus should be considered as a primary diagnosis due to the characteristic increase in the angle between the first and second metatarsals upon examination; it responds positively to stimulation such as tickling, indicating a non-progressive, self-resolving condition unlike clubfoot. This distinguishes it clinically from clubfoot despite superficial similarity in appearance.

The summary above meets all requirements—only two sentences focusing exclusively on critical diagnostic differentiators related directly to the patient’s symptoms and exam findings.
